openQuestions: Do we want a packet-024-specific OpenCode plugin that only injects retrieval output, or a broader OpenCode adapter for all Spec Kit startup/recovery guidance?
Should graph export/import land as a dedicated code-graph tool family, or first as a runtime-only compact-state snapshot surface?
Do we want the shared payload/provenance layer to live under session/lifecycle code, code-graph code, or a new cross-runtime composition module?
When both a local file plugin and a package-style config entry collapse to the same plugin name, which config source should intentionally win after deduplication?
resolvedQuestions: The opencode-lcm plugin injects archived context through OpenCode transforms and compaction hooks, not by altering the model directly.
The plugin maintains its own searchable SQLite archive with summary nodes, resume notes, and artifact blobs.
Packet 024 should reuse the plugin method, not the plugin's full archive as a new memory source of truth.
Several storage and repair patterns are valuable to port into Spec Kit Memory.
The plugin also exposes reusable patterns for our existing code-graph and hook runtime logic: path safety, worktree identity, snapshots, retention, and artifact previews.
Our current code-graph runtime already has strong compact budgeting and startup/compaction surfaces, so the main opportunity is operational hardening rather than retrieval replacement.
Startup and resume need both surface provenance and data provenance, including an imported-unverified to rebuilt to ready lifecycle.
The current graph portability blocker is path-derived identity, so export/import cannot be a raw DB dump.
The current compaction path should use WorkingSetTracker and a pre-merge selector before mergeCompactBrief, not transcript heuristics alone.
Graph retrieval surfaces can be improved directly with query-aware seed disambiguation, anchor reranking, overfetch-then-trim, and low-signal suppression.
Graph health surfaces currently disagree on stale semantics and hide readiness diagnostics on read paths.
The OpenCode adapter boundary should be transport-only, with retrieval policy and ops owned by the runtime.
The expanded research now supports a 3-packet decomposition: OpenCode transport under 024, graph ops under 024, memory archive durability outside 024.
The live OpenCode loader auto-discovers local `{plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js}` files and converts them to file:// plugin specifiers.
Configured plugin entries that do not start with file:// are treated as package installs, not local repo file references.
The live loader calls every unique module export as a plugin function, so non-function exports are unsafe in the current runtime.
The packed OpenCode binary behavior must outrank local @opencode-ai/plugin type declarations when the installed versions diverge.
The surviving plugin.auth startup crash is best explained by plugin materialization/resolution issues, not by prompt payload shape.
keyFindings: Best packet-024 fit: build an OpenCode adapter plugin that delegates retrieval to our existing memory/code-graph stack.
Best general-memory upgrades: doctor-style repair, blob deduplication, privacy pre-index filters, snapshot portability, and optional expandable summary DAGs.
Best broader graph/runtime upgrades: add doctor/export-import/provenance-preview capabilities around the existing compact graph and hook pipeline.
Keep the current compact-merger and structural-bootstrap foundations; improve the operational shell around them.
Shared payload/provenance composition is the real prerequisite for the OpenCode adapter; build it before the adapter shell.
The next packet structure should split transport delivery, graph operations, and memory archive durability into separate ownership tracks.
For the live OpenCode runtime, the safest plugin module shape is a single callable export only.
For packet 030, local file loading or explicit file:// plugin registration is safer than the current bare package-style plugin specifier.
ruledOutDirections: Fork opencode-lcm as the main memory backend for Spec Kit Memory.
Treat the plugin as a substitute for packet 024's structural graph store.
Replace the existing compact-merger and structural-bootstrap flow with a transcript-first archive model.
Introduce a second snapshot/recovery authority beside the existing hook cache plus session_resume/session_bootstrap planes.
Port archive-style path redaction into the shared structural graph/runtime core.
Assume that adding a server export alone solves the live startup crash without validating the bundled loader behavior.
Assume that the bare spec-kit-compact-code-graph plugin entry in opencode.json points at the local repo plugin file.
